segments.text:
Bioinformatics Lecture 6: 
1) Identifying protein domains continued…
2) Intro to Protein Structure Visualization

Review:
• Many tools to help find structural/fuctional domains within your protein
• No approach is guaranteed to find everything!
Easiest: Search NCBI CDD 
(conserved domain 
database) via BLAST
Most Informative (?): EBI Interpro
http://www.ebi.ac.uk/interpro/
Note: Usually doesn’t find 
domains with poor 
1°conservation
Includes searches for domains 
with poor 1°conservation:
• coiled-coil
• transmembrane regions
• disorered regions
Good idea to try >1! (different answers…)

Poorly conserved domains: useful to use additional specific tools
Coiled-coils
• Google “coiled-coil prediction”
• Range of servers, programs
• Lupas algorithm is usually sufficient
CLIP-170 results
(see Wednesday’s sequences)
1°structure: Heptad repeat
(7AA repeat)

Transmemrane domains
Most (?) useful (find from ExPASY or by search for these):
• Phobius http://phobius.sbc.su.se/ (also in Interpro, but less info)
• TMHMM http://www.cbs.dtu.dk/services/TMHMM/
• TMPred (older and less definitive)
NPC1 protein (From Wednesday’s sequences)

Disordered regions
An evolving field! Different rules, different answers…
Most useful (?) Predictors based on sequence:
• IUPRED: https://iupred2a.elte.hu/
• PONDR: http://www.pondr.com/
• MobiDB: http://mobidb.bio.unipd.it/
MobiDB presents a consensus answer for all UniProt entries using these 
tools: DisEMBL, ESpritz, GlobPlot, IUPred, Jronn, VSL2b.
Note:  Interpro presents some stripped-
down MobiDB results:
Babu Biochem Soc Trans. 2016
MobiDB results:
